sections.heading: Composition of the Board, Positions, Committees
Duties and Powers (cont.)
sections.title: Board shareholder communication and meeting procedures
sections.summary: The Board must provide equal and simultaneous information to shareholders and market participants about matters affecting share prices. It determines general meeting dates, record dates, and ensures shareholders receive necessary information. The Board ensures compliance with shareholder law, mandates attendance of all members at meetings unless justified, designates a chairperson for meetings, requires public disclosure of resolutions through the chairperson or company secretary, and mandates shareholder approval for substantial governance changes. External auditors must attend meetings and be questioned about financial statements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes procedures for shareholder communication and governance meetings that impact data transfer transparency, particularly regarding financial disclosures and auditor involvement in cross-border contexts.

requirements.text: Board must provide equal and simultaneous information to shareholders and market participants about matters influencing share price
Board determines general meeting date, record date, and ensures shareholders receive necessary information
Board ensures compliance with laws regarding general meeting and shareholder rights
Chairperson ensures all Board members attend meetings unless important reasons exist
General meetings presided by Board chairperson or vice-chairperson, or designated person
Resolutions must be publicly disclosed only through chairperson or company secretary statement
Substantial governance changes require shareholder discussion under separate agenda item
External auditor must attend meetings and be questioned about financial statements

sections.source_quote: Where appropriate, the Board shall provide all shareholders and other parties in the financial markets with equal and simultaneous information about matters that may influence the share price.

sections.heading: Duties and Powers (cont.)
sections.title: Board compensation and external roles
sections.summary: Board members' compensation is determined by general meetings, with proposals submitted by the Board. They receive reimbursement for reasonable costs incurred at meetings, approved by the chairperson. The organisation does not grant personal loans or guarantees to Board members except in usual business operations. Board members must complete induction programs covering organisational strategy, financial affairs, and responsibilities. They undergo annual training reviews and must limit other positions to ensure effective governance. Board members must disclose outside positions that could conflict with their duties and return confidential information at the end of their term.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines governance requirements for Board members that affect data transfer transparency, particularly regarding financial disclosures and conflict of interest management in cross-border contexts.

requirements.text: Board members' compensation determined by general meeting with proposals submitted by Board
Board members reimbursed for reasonable costs incurred at meetings, approved by chairperson
Organisation does not grant personal loans or guarantees to Board members except in usual business operations
Board members participate in induction program covering organisational strategy, financial affairs, and responsibilities
Board conducts annual review to identify areas requiring further training or education
Board members limit other positions to ensure effective governance duties
Board members must inform chairperson and company secretary of outside positions that may affect duties
Board members return confidential documents at end of term or guarantee disposal preserving confidentiality

sections.source_quote: The compensation of the Board members is determined by the general meeting. The Board shall submit proposals on its compensation to the general meeting.

sections.heading: and the group as a whole, as appropriate.
sections.title: Financial oversight and reporting responsibilities of the committee
sections.summary: The committee must review the quality and integrity of financial statements, including annual and half-yearly reports. It shall examine significant financial reporting issues, accounting policies, compliance with standards, unusual transactions, audit adjustments, disclosure clarity, material information, going concern status, capital adequacy, loan covenant compliance, and special documents. The committee must also review narrative reports for fairness and balance, internal controls, risk management systems, whistleblowing arrangements, fraud detection, bribery prevention, compliance procedures, internal audit functions, external audit appointments, and audit quality assurance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates rigorous oversight of financial reporting and internal controls, which impacts data transfer assessments by requiring robust governance frameworks to ensure data accuracy and integrity across international operations.

requirements.text: The Committee will examine and review the quality and integrity of the financial statements of the Company, including its annual and half-yearly reports, interim reports and any other formal announcement relating to the organisation's financial performance.

The committee shall review and report to the Board on significant financial reporting issues and judgements which these financial statements contain having regard to matters communicated to the committee by the auditor.
The committee shall review and challenge where necessary the consistency of, and any changes to, significant accounting policies both on a year on year basis and across the Company/group.
The committee shall review and challenge where necessary compliance with accounting standards, local and international, compliance with stock exchange and legal requirements.
The committee shall review and challenge where necessary the methods used to account for significant or unusual transactions where different approaches are possible.
The committee shall review and challenge where necessary significant adjustments resulting from the audit.

The committee shall review and challenge where necessary whether the Company has followed appropriate accounting standards and made appropriate estimates and judgements taking into account the views of the external auditor.
The committee shall review and challenge where necessary the clarity and completeness of disclosure in the Company's financial reports and the context in which statements are made.

The committee shall review and challenge where necessary all material information presented with the financial statements, such as the business review and the corporate governance statements relating to the audit and to risk management.
Where the committee is not satisfied with any aspect of the proposed financial reporting by the Company, it shall report its views to the Board.
The committee shall review and challenge where necessary the basis on which the organisation has been determined a going concern.
The committee shall review and challenge where necessary capital adequacy and internal controls.
The committee shall review and challenge where necessary compliance with the financial conditions of any loan covenants.
The committee shall review and challenge where necessary reviewing special documents (such as prospectuses).

Where requested by the Board, the committee shall review the content of the annual report and accounts and advise the Board on whether, taken as a whole, it is fair, balanced and understandable and provides the information necessary for shareholders and other stakeholders to assess the Company's performance, business model and strategy.

The committee shall keep under review the adequacy and effectiveness of the organisation's systems of internal control, including internal financial control and business risk management and maintaining effective internal control systems.
The committee shall review and approve the statements to be included in the annual report concerning internal controls and risk management.

The committee shall review the adequacy and security of the organisation's arrangements for its employees and contractors to raise concerns, in confidence, about possible wrongdoing in financial reporting or other matters.
The committee shall ensure that these arrangements allow proportionate and independent investigation of such matters and appropriate follow up action.
The committee shall review the Company's procedures for detecting fraud.
The committee shall review the Company's systems and controls for the prevention of bribery and receive reports on non-compliance.
The committee shall review regular reports from the Compliance Officer and keep under review the adequacy and effectiveness of the Company's compliance function.
The committee shall review significant transactions not directly related to the organisation's normal business as the committee might deem appropriate.
The committee shall review significant cases of employee conflicts of interest, misconduct or fraud, or any other unethical activity by employees or the organisation.
The Committee shall approve the appointment or termination of appointment of the head of internal audit.

The Committee shall review and approve the charter of the internal audit function and ensure the function has the necessary resources and access to information to enable it to fulfil its mandate, and is equipped to perform in accordance with appropriate professional standards for internal auditors.
The Committee shall ensure the internal auditor has direct access to the Board Chairperson and to the committee Chairperson, and is accountable to the committee.
The Committee shall review and assess the annual internal audit work plan.
The Committee shall receive a report on the results of the internal auditor's work on a periodic basis.
The Committee shall review and monitor management's responsiveness to the internal auditor's findings and recommendations.
The Committee shall meet with the head of internal audit at least once a year without the presence of management.
The Committee shall monitor and review the effectiveness of the Company's internal audit function, in the context of the Company's overall risk management system.
The Committee shall safeguard the organisation's assets against unauthorised use or disposal.

The Committee shall direct and supervise investigations into matters within its scope, for example, evaluations of the effectiveness of the organisation's internal control, cases of employee fraud, misconduct or conflict of interest.

The Committee shall consider and make recommendations to the Board, to be put to shareholders for approval at the AGM, in relation to the appointment, re-appointment and removal of the Company's external auditor.

The Committee shall ensure that at least once every ten years the audit services contract is put out to tender to enable the Committee to compare the quality and effectiveness of the services provided by the incumbent auditor with those of other audit firms.
The Committee shall investigate the issues leading to an auditor resigning and decide whether any action is required.

The Committee shall oversee the relationship with the external auditor including recommendations on their remuneration, approval of terms of engagement, assessment of independence and objectivity, satisfaction that there are no relationships affecting independence, agreement on a policy for former employees of the auditor, monitoring compliance with ethical guidance on rotation and fees, and assessing qualifications and expertise of the auditor.

The Committee shall meet regularly with the external auditor (including once at the planning stage before the audit and once after the audit at the reporting stage) and at least once a year without management being present to discuss the auditor's remit and any issues arising from the audit.

The Committee shall review and approve the annual audit plan and ensure it is consistent with the scope of the audit engagement, having regard to the seniority, expertise and experience of the audit team.
The Committee shall consider whether any significant ventures, investments or operations are not subject to external audit.
The Committee shall obtain assurance from the external auditor(s) that adequate accounting records are being maintained.

The Committee shall review the findings of the audit with the external auditor, including a discussion of major issues, key accounting and audit judgements, levels of errors identified during the audit, and the effectiveness of the audit process.
The Committee shall review any representation letter(s) requested by the external auditor before they are signed by management.
The Committee shall review the management letter and management's response to the auditor's findings and recommendations.
The Committee shall develop and implement policy on the supply of non-audit services by the external auditor to avoid threats to auditor objectivity and independence.
The Committee shall monitor the implementation of the organisation's Code of ethics.
The Committee shall review any statements on business conduct and ethical standards or requirements for the organisation and assist in developing such standards and requirements.
The Committee shall ensure compliance with the requirements of the articles of association; laws and regulations of any other applicable statute and of controlling bodies.
The Committee shall identify any violations of ethical conduct.
The Committee shall give recommendations on any potential conflict of interest or questionable situations of a material nature.

The Committee Chairperson shall report formally to the Board on its proceedings after each meeting on all matters within its duties and responsibilities and shall also formally report to the Board on how it has discharged its responsibilities.
The Committee Chairperson shall report on significant issues considered in relation to financial statements and how these were addressed.
The Committee Chairperson shall report on its assessment of the effectiveness of the external audit process and its recommendation on the appointment or reappointment of the external auditor.
The Committee shall make whatever recommendations to the Board it deems appropriate on any area within its remit where action or improvement is needed.

The Committee shall compile a report on its activities to be included in the Company's annual report, including an explanation of how the committee has addressed the effectiveness of the external audit process; the significant issues that the committee considered in relation to financial statements and how these issues were addressed.

sections.source_quote: The Committee will examine and review the quality and integrity of the financial statements of the Company, including its annual and half-yearly reports, interim reports and any other formal announcement relating to the organisation's financial performance.

sections.heading: and the group as a whole, as appropriate. (cont.)
The committee is authorised to:
sections.title: Committee authority to access employee information and professional advice
sections.summary: The committee may request information from employees to fulfill its duties, obtain independent legal or accounting advice at the organization's expense, summon employees for questioning, and publish unresolved issues in the annual report. These powers apply to the committee within the organization's governance framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section grants the committee authority to access employee information and external professional advice, which may impact data transfer assessments when handling employee data across borders or with third parties.

requirements.text: Seek any information it requires from any employee of the organization in order to perform its duties
Obtain, at the organization's expense, independent legal, accounting or other professional advice on any matter it believes it necessary to do so
Call any employee to be questioned at a meeting of the committee as and when required
Have the right to publish in the organisation's annual report, details of any issues that cannot be resolved between the committee and the Board

sections.source_quote: The committee is authorised to: 12.1 Seek any information it requires from any employee of the organisation in order to perform its duties; 12.2 Obtain, at the organisation's expense, independent legal, accounting or other professional advice on any matter it believes it necessary to do so; 1.3 Call any employee to be questioned at a meeting of the committee as and when required; 12.4 Have the right to publish in the organisation's annual report, details of any issues that cannot be resolved between the committee and the Board.

sections.heading: The committee is authorised to: (cont.)
sections.title: Board and committee evaluation and shareholder communication
sections.summary: The section mandates annual evaluation of the Board, committees, and individual directors to identify strengths and weaknesses. It requires the Nomination Committee to propose new board appointments or resignations based on evaluation results. It also mandates effective communication with shareholders, including maintaining contact with major shareholders to discuss governance, strategy, and remuneration, and ensuring shareholder views are communicated to the Board.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires annual board and committee evaluations, which impact DTIA by necessitating clear governance structures and accountability mechanisms for data handling across borders. It emphasizes communication with shareholders, which may affect data sharing practices with international stakeholders.

requirements.text: Ensure annual evaluation of Board, committees, and individual directors to identify strengths and weaknesses
Act on evaluation results by recognizing strengths and addressing weaknesses
Propose new board appointments or seek resignations through Nomination Committee
Ensure effective communication with shareholders
Maintain sufficient contact with major shareholders to understand issues and concerns
Discuss governance, strategy, and remuneration with major shareholders
Ensure shareholder views are communicated to the Board
Arrange for Chairperson of Board committees to be available at AGM
Ensure all directors attend AGM

sections.source_quote: Ensuring the performance of the Board, its committees and individual directors is evaluated at least once a year and acting on the results of such evaluation by recognising the strengths and addressing the weaknesses of the Board. Where appropriate, through the Nomination Committee, proposing that new members be appointed to the Board or seeking the resignation of others.

sections.heading: The committee is authorised to: (cont.)
sections.title: Non-executive director responsibilities and induction
sections.summary: The section outlines the responsibilities of non-executive directors, including constructive challenge to strategy development, scrutiny of management performance, financial information accuracy, and remuneration determination. It also covers director induction, including role clarification, company policies, and procedures for handling conflicts of interest and confidentiality.
sections.dtia_summary: This section impacts DTIA by defining non-executive director responsibilities that may affect data handling practices across borders, particularly through requirements for conflict of interest disclosures and confidentiality protocols.

requirements.text: Non-executive directors should constructively challenge and contribute to strategy development
Non-executive directors should scrutinize management performance against goals
Non-executive directors should ensure financial information accuracy and robust risk management
Non-executive directors determine appropriate executive remuneration levels
Non-executive directors have a prime role in appointing and removing senior management
Non-executive directors should disclose potential conflicts of interest to Chairperson and Secretary
All information acquired during appointment is confidential and must not be released without prior clearance
Organisation provides comprehensive formal induction within first month

sections.source_quote: Non-executive directors should constructively challenge and contribute to the development of strategy; Non-executive directors should scrutinise the performance of management in meeting agreed goals and objectives and monitor the reporting of performance; Non-executive directors should satisfy themselves that financial information is accurate and that financial controls and systems of risk management are robust and defensible; Non-executive directors are responsible for determining appropriate levels of remuneration of executive directors and have a prime role in appointing, and where necessary, removing senior management and in succession planning.

sections.heading: The committee is authorised to: (cont.)
sections.title: Board committee requirements for governance and ethics
sections.summary: This section outlines specific requirements for board committees including remuneration, nomination, and other committees. It mandates that committees define their roles, link policies to strategic objectives, identify members, establish meeting schedules, and review regulatory requirements and market practices. The section also requires technical training tailored to financial expertise levels and addresses current issues and investor perspectives.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes governance requirements that may impact data transfer if committees handle sensitive financial data, but does not directly address cross-border data transfers or international data flows.

requirements.text: Define role and remit of the committee
Link committee policy to organisation's strategic objectives
Identify members of the committee and those regularly invited to attend meetings
Establish meeting schedule with pro forma forward agendas
Review regulatory and legal requirements in Mauritius and relevant jurisdictions
Assess market practice, current trends and best practice guidelines
Address current issues
Gather views of investors on current arrangements and potential areas of focus
Hold meetings with CFO, external auditors and head of internal audit
Provide technical training on key matters tailored to financial expertise level
Hold meetings with remuneration advisers
Provide technical training on key matters tailored to experience level

sections.source_quote: viii. Current issues. ix. Views of investors on current arrangements and potential areas of focus. x. Meeting(s) with the CFO, external auditors and the head of internal audit. xi. Technical training on key matters, tailored according to level of financial expertise.

sections.heading: The committee is authorised to: (cont.)
sections.title: Corporate governance scorecard framework for self-assessment
sections.summary: This section describes a scorecard framework for self-assessment of corporate governance practices. It explains that scorecards measure adherence to voluntary governance codes rather than regulatory compliance, and outlines their purpose in assessing governance practices, tracking progress, and enabling comparisons between companies. The framework emphasizes the use of scorecards by various stakeholders including companies, regulators, and investors to improve governance quality.
sections.dtia_summary: This section provides a framework for governance self-assessment that may indirectly affect data transfer practices through improved governance standards, but does not directly address cross-border data transfers or international data flows.

requirements.text: Scorecards measure observance of corporate governance codes
Scorecards assess governance practices over time
Scorecards compare governance practices between companies and countries
Scorecards encourage improvement of governance practices

sections.source_quote: A scorecard is a quantitative tool to measure the level of observance of a corporate governance Code.

sections.heading: The committee is authorised to: (cont.)
sections.title: Corporate governance references and frameworks
sections.summary: This section lists references to corporate governance frameworks, including the King III Report, Mauritius Institute of Directors publications, OECD guidelines, and World Bank reports. It provides a foundation for understanding governance standards but does not contain specific operational requirements or obligations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section lists references to governance frameworks that may inform data transfer practices but does not directly impact DTIA requirements.

requirements.text: List of corporate governance references including King III Report
Mauritius Institute of Directors publications on ethics and governance
OECD corporate governance guidelines
World Bank reports on corporate governance observance

sections.source_quote: King M. 2009. The King III Report. Johannesburg: Institute of Directors Southern Africa.
